▲ Page top
haste
QuoteReply
# Sep 05 2002 at 12:20 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592758597714
3 posts
all this talk about haste not effecting delay of a weapon...

here is an example you might not of thought about

consider a weapon with delay of 20, say a dagger 9/20, lets say the person wielding it had no haste but got hit with a 100percent attack SLOW spell, would that mean he cant swing the weapon? no... it means it now turns into a 9/40, or the same as if he was wielding a 9/40 weapon.

now lets say this same person gets a haste item and chanter buffs to increase his attack speed 100percent, would that mean its a 9/0 weapon? no..

that means it would attack the same speed as if he was wielding a 9/10. a 9/10 weapon is 100percent faster then a 9/20

still confused?

okay lets say the same person who got slowed 100percent now wielding at 9/40, and suddenly puts on haste items and haste buffs which negate the 100percent slow, does that mean now he is at 9/0? hehe no, it means he is back at 9/20

100percent haste will cut weapon delay in half,

so to put into an equation

new delay = old delay x (haste percentage/200)

100percent is .5
50 percent is .25
25 percent is .125

first you have to divide the haste percent by 200, then multiply the result by the original weapon delay, take the result of that and subtract it from original weapon delay to get new hasted delay.


so if you have a 45/60 weapon, and are wearing a 21 percent haste item it would look like this

21/200 = .105

multiply the 60 delay by the .105 = 6.3

subtract the 6.3 from the original delay of 60 = 53.7 so your cleaver with a fbss (if fbss is 21 percent haste) is now a 45/53.7 or 45/54 for simplicity

if you get 100percent hasted via items and spell/song buffs, then you could bring it down to 45/30

so yes, haste directly effects weapon delay. the flaw that most people assume is 100percent decrease in delay = 0 when in reality 100percent decrease cuts the delay exactly in half

100percent increase in delay (via slow) doubles weapon delay, 100percent decrease (via haste) halves the delay

if your still confused, think of taking a trip to another town going 50mph which takes you 1 hour and your friend is gonna go 100percent faster, does that mean he takes no time at all to get there? hehe no, it means he will get there in 30mins, effectively reducing trip time in half, or your weapon delay in half :)
▲ Page top
RE: haste

# Sep 22 2003 at 2:47 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1632097023438

When I first got this weapon, I thought it was everything I ever wanted (this does crazy dmg/sec & looks cool equiped). But, alas, I've finally gotten into the NO DROPS & need to sell.

At 53 (SK), it has a dmg bonus of 28. After using it for half a year, I still haven't seen anyone else wielding one.

If anyone is interested on Prexus server, send me a tell w/ an offer.

Big Hits
QuoteReply
# Jul 26 2001 at 7:39 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250114678
6 posts
This hits hard..I gave it to my level 20 warrior..I was rolling 90+ hits right off the bat..I had a Crit of 158..I don't think level 20 wizards can nuke for that much.

I can't wait till my warrior gets a few levels under his belt. I have heard of Ungodly big hits at higher levels. 500+ Crits and 700+ Crips.

You can't believe the difference going from 30+ hits at Level 19 to 90+ hits at level 20.

I am lucky to have this beast of a weapon.
▲ Page top
RE: Big Hits
QuoteReply
# Sep 10 2002 at 8:44 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591764012611
30 posts
The one level difference is because you can't hit for anything over 30 before level 20, excluding crits and crips. This is to prevent ultra-twinkage, where the guy just gives his level 10 character a bunch of awesome stuff and then sends him off to fight things twice his level.
▲ Page top
This + Haste Item
QuoteReply
# Jul 24 2001 at 3:46 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591759413183
58 posts
Just think of this with any type of haste item, say a FBSS 21% haste, that makes this a 44/47 roughly, already that makes it the best weapon in the game, dmg/dly ratio wise. Now say you have a CoF 36% haste, that makes this a 44/38.5 or so. Now it is THE BEST weapon in the game, hands down. Now the delay isn't even that bad, compared to the 60 of before. I would gladly get this for my twink warrior, that is still in the planning stage, but I say grats to anyone that has it.

Just to clear something up, unhasted and with the advent of Velious, there are about 30-35 2HS weapons that are far superior to this item, for instance, the Primal Velium 2HS is 33/35. Just thought I'd clear that up.



▲ Page top
RE: This + Haste Item
QuoteReply
# Aug 07 2001 at 6:22 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591750156669
Scholar
101 posts
Just a clarification: The actual ratio with CoF would be about 44/50 (still impressive...), and with max Haste (say with CoF and Augmentation) it would be around 44/42 or so. Haste isn't the percentage off your delay, it's the increase in your attack speed. For example, if there were such a thing as a 100% Haste item (and the Haste cap were removed), it wouldn't let you attack with a delay of 0, it would double your attack rate.

Grygus, Shaman of Halas
▲ Page top
RE: This + Haste Item
